message:
Has your cat ever locked eyes with you from across the room and then slooowly closed and opened them, almost like a lazy, peaceful little wink? 😻 That isn't your cat being sleepy — it's one of the sweetest things they can tell you.

Animal behavior scientists at the University of Sussex actually put this to the test. They found that when people gave their cats a slow blink, the cats were far more likely to slow-blink right back. And here's the heart-melting part: when a total stranger slow-blinked at an unfamiliar cat, that cat was more willing to come closer and trust them.

Researchers believe a slow blink is a cat's way of saying "I feel safe with you." Closing their eyes around you means they don't feel the need to keep watch — a huge compliment from a little creature built to stay alert. Plenty of us lovingly call it a kitty kiss. 💛

So next time your cat catches your eye, try giving them a soft, slow blink and see if they answer you back. Have you ever gotten a slow-blink from your cat? Tell me about your little sweetheart below! 🐾
